# cfs driver catalog

Each backend is a **mount** in the paths registry (RFD Â§3). Every node maps onto one of four archetypes (Blob / Relational / Append / ObjectGraph) and declares which universal verbs it supports â€” **unsupported verbs are rejected at parse time** (RFD Â§5), so the AI never plans a rejected op. This catalog is generated from the binary's describe surface; it cannot drift from the drivers.

## Drivers

### `/drive` â€” BlobNamespace

- example node: `/drive/Reports`
- native verbs: ls cp mv rm (+ universal upsert/remove)

Universal verbs (âś“ supported / âś— rejected at parse time):

| verb | supported |
|------|-----------|
| `SELECT` | âś— |
| `INSERT` | âś— |
| `UPSERT` | âś— |
| `UPDATE` | âś— |
| `REMOVE` | âś— |
| `LS` | âś— |
| `CP` | âś— |
| `MV` | âś— |
| `RM` | âś— |

Procedures (`CALL drive.action(..)`):

| procedure | params | irreversible |
|-----------|--------|--------------|
| `copy` | file_id: Text, parent_id: Text, name: Text | no |

Prelude aliases: _none_

Pushdown: where=true project=false limit=true order=false join=false aggregate=false distinct=false group_by=false

### `/github` â€” ObjectGraphWorkflow

- example node: `/github/o/r/pulls`
- native verbs: SELECT INSERT UPDATE REMOVE + CALL driver.action

Universal verbs (âś“ supported / âś— rejected at parse time):

| verb | supported |
|------|-----------|
| `SELECT` | âś“ |
| `INSERT` | âś“ |

| `RM` | âś— |

Procedures (`CALL github.action(..)`):

| procedure | params | irreversible |
|-----------|--------|--------------|
| `merge` | method: Text, sha: Text | yes |
| `dispatch` | workflow: Text, ref: Text, inputs: Json | yes |
| `review` | event: Text, body: Text | no |

- example node: `/slack/ws/#general/messages`
- native verbs: SELECT(tail) INSERT(append)

Universal verbs (âś“ supported / âś— rejected at parse time):

| verb | supported |

| `pin` | channel: Text, ts: Text | yes |
| `unpin` | channel: Text, ts: Text | no |
| `update` | channel: Text, ts: Text, text: Text | no |
| `delete` | channel: Text, ts: Text | yes |

## Codecs (the shared `DECODE`/`ENCODE` registry, RFD Â§4)

Codecs bridge blob â†” relational independent of driver identity. Builtin formats:

- `csv`
- `json`
- `jsonl`
- `md+frontmatter`
- `toml`
- `yaml`
